title: Research Interests
text:
My research focuses on applying statistics, data science, geographic information systems (GIS), and software engineering to improve property assessment and property tax administration. I am particularly interested in developing practical tools that help assessors make more informed, efficient, and transparent decisions while preserving the importance of professional jedgement.

Assessment Data Quality
Reliable assessment begins with reliable data. I am interested in methods for automatically identifying inconsistencies, missing information, and unusual records that may affect valuation accuracy, ratio studies, and taxpayer confidencce.


Assessment Equity
A fundamental goal of property assessment is to ensure that similar properties are assessed in a fair and equitable manner. My interests include statistical methods for evaluating assessment equity, identifying patterns of inequity, and developing tools that help assessment professionals monitor compliance with accepted standards.


Ratio Studies and Performance Measurement
Ratio studies are among the most important tools for evaluating assessment quality, yet many offices continue to rely on labor-intensive workflows. I am interested in methods for automating ratio studies, improving data visualization, and making statistical results more accessible to both assessment professionals and the public.


Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
Location is one of the most important determinants of property value. I am interested in applying GIS to neighborhood analysis, market visualization, spatial statistics, and the communication of assessment information.


Data Science and Statistical Modeling
Modern statistical methods offer opportunities to better understand real estate markets and support mass appraisal. My interests include regression analysis, machine learning, time adjustment estimation, anomaly detection, and other analytical techniques that can assist assessment professionals in making well-supported decisions.


Decision-Support Software
Rather than replacing professional judgement, I believe software should augment and enhance it. I am interested in designing practical tools that automate repetitive tasks, improve data quality, generate reports, and help assessment professionals focus their expertise where it adds the greatest value.


Current Research
I am currently interviewing assessment professionals to better understand the operational challenges faced by assessor offices. These conversations are helping identify opportunities where modern analytics and thoughtfully designed software can improve efficiency, consistency, and transparency throughout the assessment process.


Collaboration
I welcome conversations with assessors, appraisers, GIS professionals, data analysts, researchers, and others working in property tax administration. If your organization is interested in discussing assessment workflows, data challenges, or opportunities for collaboration, I would be pleased to connect.

title: About
text:
I have worked in the property appraisal and assessment profession since graduating from college. My academic background includes a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration with an emphasis in Economics and a minor in Mathematics, which sparked my interest in applying quantitative methods to real-world public policy and valuation challenges.
Professionally, I work in property tax administration and have experience in mass appraisal, assessment analytics, ratio studies, geographic information systems (GIS), and statistical analysis. My work has led me to appreciate both the complexity of property assessment and the importance of producing fair, equitable, and transparent valuations.
Outside of my professional responsibilities, I enjoy exploring how statistics, data science, GIS, and software engineering can improve assessment workflows. My current interests include assessment data quality, ratio study automation, assessment equity analysis, valuation modeling, and decision-support tools for assessors. Through this website, I document my research, share technical articles, and connect with other professionals interested in advancing the field of property assessment.
Beyond my work in the public sector, I volunteer at my local church, where I manage the bookstore. I enjoy learning, teaching, and contributing to projects that serve both my profession and my community.

Areas of Expertise

Property Assessment & Mass Appraisal
Assessment Equity & Ratio Studies
Economics & Statistics
GIS & Spatial Analysis
Python, R, SQL, and Data Visualization
Assessment Data Quality & Analytics

title: Connor Marriott
text:
Property Assessment • Statistics • GIS • Data Analytics
Exploring how modern analytics, statistics, and software can improve property assessment, mass appraisal, and property tax administration.

My interests lie at the intersection of property assessment, statistics, geographic information systems (GIS), and software development. This website documents my research into improving assessment workflows through better data, analytics, and decision-support tools.
